meaning: n.沉重行走的人,辛勤工作的人
examples: They see you as very cautious, extremely careful, a slow and steady plodder.
He tries to play 10 matches a year, but is frustrated by being "a plodder, a grafter, slow but sure... you're either good or you're bad," he MUSES as our time draws to a close.
The Reverend William Thornton who blogs on Southern Baptist Convention issues under the title SBC Plodder is the man.
